response.justification: Exceptional fit for AI-native engineering context with current hands-on MCP and agentic-workflow experience at re:cinq. Strong TypeScript, architectural versatility, and demonstrated ownership across full-stack roles. However, critical gap: no evidence of Python expertise despite being listed as must-have. Recent promotion to Staff level (1 month) leaves track record at strategic level unproven. Recommend interview to assess Python capability and strategic execution at Staff scope.
strengths: Currently working on MCP frameworks and agentic workflows at re:cinq—direct, hands-on experience in the exact AI-native engineering environment the role operates in

Strong TypeScript and full-stack architectural expertise across web, mobile, desktop, embedded; built multiple high-scale platforms (direct mail millions annually, high-throughput data systems handling millions of requests/sec)
Proven ownership mindset and technical craftsmanship emphasis (TDD practitioner, founded GISCollective, led infrastructure/DevOps setups, contributed across entire stack)
concerns: No demonstrated Python expertise despite being listed as must-have strong skill; resume shows 10+ languages but Python absent
Staff-level role very recent (1 month at re:cinq); prior roles titled Senior Developer, leaving unclear track record on strategic technical decision-making and stakeholder coordination at Staff scale

Distributed systems experience present (microservices, high-throughput) but lacks explicit articulation of depth in consensus, fault tolerance, replication, partitioning—core Staff-level concerns for high-reliability systems
